perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, both parties, for their mutual benefit, desire to have the State disclose to Vendor certain specifications, designs, plans, drawings or other business and/or technical information which is proprietary, restricted or secret, and confidential to the State (“CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION").
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, both parties, for their mutual benefit, desire to have the State disclose to Vendor certain specifications, designs, plans, drawings or other business and/or technical information which is considered public knowledge but handled with discretion, and confidential to the State (“CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION").
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by defining 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' as being both 'public knowledge' and 'confidential'. This creates uncertainty about the actual nature of the information being protected, which contradicts the other sections of the text that expect the information to be secret or restricted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor shall use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION only for the purpose of discussing street level route information for the State’s fiber optic network data points and paths with the State’s support team in discussions regarding the State’s fiber project. Vendor shall restrict disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees with a need to know (and advise such employees of the obligations assumed herein), and shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party without prior written approval of the State. These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: i. which is independently developed by Vendor or is lawfully received free of restriction from another source having the right to so furnish such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was known to Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text defines 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' as being 'public knowledge but handled with discretion, and confidential'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes that 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' is secret and restricted. Specifically, the contradicted text states that the Vendor must 'restrict disclosure' and not disclose to any third party, which is incompatible with the changed text defining the information as 'public knowledge'. The contradiction is strong because it introduces a fundamental conflict regarding the nature and treatment of the information, making the confidentiality obligations unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor shall restrict disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees with a need to know (and advise such employees of the obligations assumed herein), and shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party without prior written approval of the State.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor may disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees, contractors, and consultants without restriction. Vendor shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party competitors without prior written approval of the State.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by allowing unrestricted disclosure to employees, contractors, and consultants, while the original clause restricts disclosure to only employees with a 'need to know'. This makes it unclear who is allowed to access the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and under what circumstances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor may disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees, contractors, and consultants without restriction. Vendor shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party competitors without prior written approval of the State. These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: i. which is independently developed by Vendor or is lawfully received free of restriction from another source having the right to so furnish such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was known to Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'need to know' restriction for employees, contractors, and consultants regarding CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The contradicted text duplicates this changed text, which creates a contradiction with the original requirement that employees only have access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION on a 'need to know' basis. This qualifies as an inconsistency creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined, because the change breaks compatibility between sections of the original contract, creating confusion regarding who has access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and under what circumstances.

perturbation.original_text: These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: i. which is independently developed by Vendor or is lawfully received free of restriction from another source having the right to so furnish such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was known to Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.changed_text: These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: i. which is independently developed by Vendor, even if it contains elements derived from the State's CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public with or without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was reasonably believed by Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State orally represents is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces contradictions by: (i) allowing independently developed information to contain elements derived from the State's CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, effectively undermining the confidentiality obligation. (ii) removing the condition that the information becomes publicly available *without* breach of the agreement, meaning it could be publicly available due to the Vendor's breach. (iii) changing the condition to information 'reasonably believed by vendor to be free of restriction', to 'the State orally represents is free of such restrictions'. This creates uncertainty about the exceptions to confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor may disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to its employees, contractors, and consultants without restriction. Vendor shall not disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any third party competitors without prior written approval of the State. These restrictions on the use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: i. which is independently developed by Vendor, even if it contains elements derived from the State's CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; ii. after it has become generally available to the public with or without breach of this Agreement by Vendor; iii. which, at the time of disclosure to Vendor, was reasonably believed by Vendor to be free of restriction; or iv. which the State orally represents is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces contradictions as explained. Allowing independently developed information to contain elements derived from the State's CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION directly conflicts with the intent to keep such information confidential. Changing the condition of information becoming public to 'with or without breach' undermines the Vendor's confidentiality obligations. Additionally, the change from information 'known to Vendor to be free of restriction' to 'the State orally represents is free of such restrictions' introduces uncertainty, creating an in-text contradiction that impacts the enforceability and clarity of the contract regarding confidential information, as it suggests oral representations are sufficient, contradicting the need for written approval.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of this Agreement with respect to the disclosure and use of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of last disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of this Agreement with respect to the disclosure and use of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall terminate immediately upon the conclusion of the project discussions.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the obligations regarding the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION terminate immediately after the project discussions, while the original clause states that they survive for three years from the date of last disclosure. This creates a direct conflict regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of this Agreement with respect to the disclosure and use of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall terminate immediately upon the conclusion of the project discussions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the confidentiality obligations terminate immediately upon the conclusion of the project discussions. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, also states that the confidentiality obligations terminate immediately upon the conclusion of the project discussions. The original text stated that the obligations survive for three years. Thus, the change from 'three years' to 'immediately' causes the contradiction, because the change implies termination immediately while the original text dictates termination after 3 years.
